description_long: This house features a long, rectangular layout built into a sloped terrain, with a multi-sectioned brown gable roof and white exterior walls. The structure includes several dormer windows on the upper floor, and a series of arched openings on the lower level, likely serving as a garage or covered walkway. A prominent staircase leads up to the main entrance, which is centrally located and flanked by windows and a small porch area. The property is bordered by green landscaping on the upper side and a retaining wall on the lower side, emphasizing its adaptation to the hillside. The windows are arranged in mostly symmetrical rows, with some variation in size and placement, and the roofline is punctuated by chimneys and skylights. The overall design suggests a blend of traditional and functional architectural elements, suitable for a residential neighborhood.
description: A hillside house with a brown gable roof, white walls, dormer windows, arched lower openings, and a central staircase entry, set into landscaped terrain.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the house, brown gable roof, white walls, and the sloped terrain with retaining wall.
description_view_1: End view highlighting the stepped roofline, arched lower openings, and the slope of the property.
description_view_2: Front view displaying the main entry staircase, symmetrical dormer windows, arched garage or walkway, and landscaped areas.
description_view_3: Opposite end view showing the rear side of the house, roofline, and a small staircase leading to a secondary entrance.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical rows with dormers and arched lower windows
attribute_entryway_design: Central staircase entry with small porch, steps, and arched openings below
